_about: Phase 4.6 head-to-head subset selection. 12 images, balanced across (a) clear wins for our pipeline, (b) clear losses, (c) surprises where stage-B refinement hurts vs stage A alone, (d) coverage across categories and challenge tags. Selection driven by outputs/eval/leaderboard_phase3.csv at 5x; rendering happens at 4x only because suppixel.ai (SUPIR/HYPIR) caps at 4x — see _scope_note below.
_scope_note: Suppixel.ai's SUPIR and HYPIR tools support upscaling at 1x/2x/3x/4x only. 5x and 10x are out of scope for the head-to-head — this matches the reality of public diffusion-SR tools (Real-ESRGAN, x4-upscaler, SUPIR are all 4x native). Phase 7 writeup frames 4x as the apples-to-apples comparison ratio, and our pipeline's 5x/10x results as beyond-SOTA exploration with no available reference model.

subset.context: Our two_stage at 5x beats Real-ESRGAN by 0.101 LPIPS - the LARGEST win in the test set. Diffusion's invented texture matches the original heavy grain better than Real-ESRGAN's denoising. SUPIR should also handle this well; HYPIR comparison interesting.
Two_stage beats Real-ESRGAN by 0.047 LPIPS at 5x - the second of only two clear wins. Text legibility is a well-known SUPIR strength; this image is the head-to-head's flagship text case.

Two_stage's WORST loss vs Real-ESRGAN at 5x (delta +0.320 LPIPS). Ornate Dubai facade with dense repeating geometry - Real-ESRGAN's GAN extracts the structure cleanly while diffusion hallucinates unstable texture. SUPIR is the SOTA reference for exactly this kind of image; expect a dramatic SUPIR win.

Top-2 loss for two_stage (delta +0.268). Iconic Yosemite Falls landscape - clean bicubic LR has all the structure Real-ESRGAN needs, so diffusion's invented detail is net-negative. Useful as a 'flagship landscape where dedicated SR wins' image.

Stage A (x4-upscaler alone) hits LPIPS 0.20; stage B refinement WORSENS this to 0.33 - a 0.13 regression from our own pipeline's earlier component. Eye-detail at extreme zoom is a classic SUPIR test case; comparison should be visually striking.
Stage A 0.258, two_stage 0.361, Real-ESRGAN 0.268. Stage B refinement adds invented foliage that hurts LPIPS. Landmark landscape, intermediate-difficulty case for SUPIR.
Second fine_architecture case (with Dubai). Pre-fire Notre-Dame ornate facade. Worth seeing whether Dubai's outcome generalises to other ornate-architecture content or is content-specific.

Pine canopy / foliage. SUPIR's reported strength is exactly this kind of high-frequency natural texture - canonical test case for how SUPIR handles micro-detail invention vs our pipeline's softer treatment.
Water reflection - a category where two_stage loses heavily (delta +0.234 vs Real-ESRGAN). Symmetric structure (real + reflected) tests how each method handles consistent geometry.

Night-sky landscape. Our stage-B LoRA actually IMPROVED night scenes (62.5% win rate per Phase 4c eval), so this is the one image where the LoRA flavour might matter. Comparison: SUPIR vs HYPIR vs our LoRA-augmented two_stage on night content.

Owl portrait - feathers + eyes are an SR favorite. x4-upscaler alone hits LPIPS 0.18 (very good); two_stage regresses to 0.27. Real-ESRGAN at 0.22. Tight three-way race makes this a useful comparator vs SUPIR/HYPIR.
NYC daytime cityscape - mixed skyscraper structure + windows + sky. Coverage for the cityscape subcategory; NOT a fine_architecture-tagged image, so distinct from Dubai/Notre Dame.
